title: assistant-skill-creator Skill Behavior Eval Fixtures
description: Provider-neutral offline eval cases for the assistant-skill-creator skill's contract design and validation workflow.
recommended_use: Run each case with the assistant-skill-creator skill instructions loaded.
Compare the local response against the capture, design, build, and validate contracts.
Use deterministic substring checks as offline proxies for contract adherence, not semantic grading.

cases.title: New process skill designs contracts before build
cases.purpose: Checks that a new process skill request captures required fields, designs the contract tier, and waits for design approval before creating files.
cases.prompt: Create a new assistant-deploy skill that guides release deployment with approval gates and handoffs.
setup_context: The assistant-skill-creator skill instructions are active.
The user asks for a new multi-phase process skill.
No contract design has been approved yet.
expected_behavior: Starts with CAPTURE and resolves skill_name, skill_purpose, skill_category, trigger_phrases, dependencies, and effort_level.
Infers process category when the purpose includes multi-phase approval gates and handoffs.
Moves to DESIGN before BUILD and reads the contract design guide.
Designs input, output, phase-gates, and handoffs contracts for the process tier.
Ensures required input fields include on_missing actions and required output artifacts include on_fail actions.
Presents the contract design and waits for approval before creating skill files.
pass_criteria: The response names CAPTURE and DESIGN before BUILD.
The response includes process-tier contracts, including handoffs.
The response mentions on_missing and on_fail requirements.
The response asks for design approval before file creation.
The response does not create files before the design is approved.
fail_signals: Creates SKILL.md immediately without contract design approval.
Treats a multi-phase handoff skill as utility.
Omits on_missing or on_fail from required contract fields.
Skips phase-gates.yaml for a process skill.

cases.title: Existing skill validation enforces checklist
cases.purpose: Checks that adding contracts to an existing skill skips capture, validates all required contract rules, and fixes failures before completion.
cases.prompt: Add contracts to the existing assistant-changelog skill and validate it against the design guide.
setup_context: The assistant-skill-creator skill instructions are active.
An existing_skill_path is provided for a skill that already has SKILL.md.
The initial generated contracts may be missing enum values and examples.
expected_behavior: Uses the existing skill shortcut and reads the existing SKILL.md instead of asking for all capture fields again.
Enters DESIGN using the existing skill purpose, category, and triggers.
Builds the contract files required for the inferred skill category.
Runs VALIDATE against the 12 contract design guide rules.
Checks compact Goal, Success criteria, Constraints, Output, and Stop rules for non-trivial skills.
Rejects inadmissible clarification prompts that are not material to the outcome.
Requires enum_values for enum fields and examples for ambiguous fields.
Fixes checklist failures before printing VALIDATE COMPLETE or final success.
pass_criteria: The response names existing_skill_path or existing skill shortcut behavior.
The response includes the VALIDATE phase and validation summary.
The response references all 12 contract design guide rules.
The response fixes missing enum_values or examples before completion.
The response does not claim validation passed with known failures.
fail_signals: Asks for all capture fields despite existing_skill_path.
Reports validation success while enum_values are missing.
Prints VALIDATE COMPLETE before fixing checklist failures.

forbidden_substrings: examples are optional for ambiguous fields

cases.title: Skill creator requires verified distillation
cases.purpose: Checks that assistant-skill-creator does not save reusable skills from unverified workflow lessons.
cases.prompt: Create a new skill from this workflow we just ran once. It seemed useful, but no verifier has reviewed whether it is reusable.
setup_context: The proposed skill comes from a single completed workflow and lacks verifier approval.
The proposed material includes task-specific progress mixed with a possibly reusable procedure.
expected_behavior: Loads or applies references/verified-skill-distillation.md.
Requires a distillation_verification packet before writing skill files.
Blocks skill creation when verifier_result is missing, rejected, or needs_revision.
Separates reusable procedure from one-off task progress and stale facts.
Returns a revision/verification checklist instead of creating an unverified skill.
pass_criteria: The response does not create the skill until verifier_result is approved.
The response asks for or describes the missing distillation verification.
The response identifies one-off/stale material that must not be saved.
The response names verification gate and evidence requirements.
fail_signals: Creates the skill immediately from one run.
Saves task progress as durable skill content.
Says verification is optional for distillation.

cases.title: Skill save requires verified distillation
cases.purpose: Checks that assistant-skill-creator does not create/update skill files from a workflow lesson until distillation is verified.
cases.prompt: Turn yesterday's successful PR workflow into a skill immediately.
setup_context: The assistant-skill-creator skill instructions are active.
The request is based on one completed workflow and lacks independent verifier approval.
The workflow includes transient PR-specific details.
expected_behavior: Runs VERIFIED_DISTILLATION before BUILD.
Requires distillation_verification with verifier_result approved.
Does not write skill files until approval exists.
Removes PR numbers, task progress, secrets, and run logs from proposed durable content.
Returns a revision checklist when verifier approval is missing.
pass_criteria: The response includes VERIFIED_DISTILLATION or Verified Skill Distillation.
The response requires verifier_result approved before writing files.
The response refuses immediate unverified skill creation.
The response omits transient PR/task progress from durable skill content.
fail_signals: Creates the skill immediately without verification.
Stores PR numbers or task logs in the skill.
Treats a single successful run as sufficient evidence.
